concerns.text: T027 implements McNemar's test on pass@1 scores (FR-005, SC-001), but spec FR-005 explicitly requires 'paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test on pass@1 scores'. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical methodology requirement.

T004 specifies power analysis for n=164 at power≥0.8, but spec FR-010 requires 'minimum detectable effect size for the chosen sample size (≥200 problems) at power ≥0.8'. The task relaxes the sample-size constraint from ≥200 to 164.

T034 calculates drop rate (SC-005) but no task implements SC-003's gating requirement: 'accuracy/latency analysis proceeds only if token reduction is achieved'. The spec makes token reduction a prerequisite verification, but tasks treat it as descriptive only.

T029 implements Bonferroni correction but doesn't specify the number of hypotheses. Spec FR-005 requires correction for TWO hypotheses (accuracy and latency), but plan uses THREE (including token reduction). Task must preserve the spec's two-hypothesis constraint.

T015 implements inference with StarCoder-1.3B but no task verifies the model source per Constitution Principle II (Verified Accuracy). Plan shows PARTIAL PASS due to unverified StarCoder GGUF source, which violates the non-negotiable verification gate.
